commands.review_summary:
Adds ClawScan digest state and rollup storage, lifecycle syncing, staff Convex/HTTP read APIs, a management security scan overview, clawhub-mod security-scans commands, docs, specs, and a repo-local operator skill.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The available verification is feature proof and tests, not a current-main failure reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer/author rebase plus rollout/backfill approval, not a narrow automated repair on this review pass.

target.title: feat: add ClawScan security scan visibility
target.body: ## Summary - Add ClawScan-first digest tables, rollups, lifecycle updates, backfills, and staff read APIs for security scan visibility across skills and plugins. - Add the `/management` security scan overview with verdict/pipeline/time-window rollups, failed/queued/running drilldowns, and artifact detail inspection. - Add `clawhub-mod security-scans` commands plus the repo-local `security-scan-overview` skill. - Document the ClawScan/Codex source-of-truth model, SkillSpector evidence role, scale tradeoffs, API endpoints, and operator workflows. ## Screenshots Published UI proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/pull/2403#issuecomment-4539161615 **Management security overview** **Artifact drilldown** **Mobile overview** ## Performance / Scale Considerations These were verified in the implementation and docs, not just PR prose: - **No live aggregation over source artifact tables for the dashboard.** Staff visibility reads from `securityScanArtifactStates`, `securityScanCurrentRollups`, and `securityScanHourlyRollups` instead of aggregating `skills`, `skillVersions`, `packages`, `packageReleases`, and `securityScanJobs` on every request. - **Indexed current-state reads.** The schema adds indexes for artifact kind + artifact key, verdict, scan job status, failure status, ClawScan category, and display name, so list/drilldown views can stay on targeted indexes. - **Narrow rollup rows instead of arbitrary analytics.** Current rollups are keyed by artifact kind, rollup kind/category, ClawScan verdict, pipeline status, and failure status. Hourly rollups are bounded to recent time-window views rather than becoming a general history warehouse. - **Idempotent hourly event accounting.** `securityScanHourlyRollupEvents` records source event keys so replayed lifecycle writes do not double-count hourly scan events. - **Pagination and hard caps.** Artifact lists paginate with a max limit of 100, overview windows clamp to 168 hours, failed samples clamp to 50, current rollup reads cap at 500 rows, hourly reads cap at 2,000 rows per kind, evidence snippets cap at 10, and digest backfill pages clamp to 250 rows. - **Cursor-based repair path.** Backfills page through active skills/plugins with indexed queries and update current rollups by applying deltas from previous state to next state. Stale digest pruning and current-rollup rebuild paths exist for repair rather than requiring full-table dashboard reads. - **Existing stack only.** This keeps the work inside the existing Convex/backend + HTTP/CLI surfaces and does not introduce new database technology or a separate analytics store. The tradeoff: this is optimized for operational questions like "what is current?", "what happened recently?", "what is queued/failed?", and "what happened to this artifact?" It is intentionally not optimized for open-ended historical BI queries. ## What We Left Out - **Post-deploy production proof.** Prod auth works (`bun run mod -- whoami` passed), but the new `security-scans` routes are not deployed yet, so prod overview/list/inspect smoke needs to run after merge and manual deploy. - **Alerting/notifications.** This PR gives staff visibility, but does not add thresholds, paging, Slack/Discord alerts, or scheduled digest anomaly reports. - **Arbitrary historical slicing/export.** The dashboard supports bounded recent windows, not long-retention analytics, unlimited exports, or multi-dimensional exploration across every scanner signal. - **Full per-artifact scan history UI.** Drilldown shows current digest state, latest job metadata, and compact evidence summaries. It does not expose every historical scan attempt or raw worker artifact. - **New moderation mutations.** The UI is read-only for this slice and preserves existing role gates. It does not add new bulk actions, quarantine controls, or rescan policy changes beyond existing scanner flows. - **Automatic production rollout/backfill orchestration.** Backfill/repair functions are implemented, but this PR does not schedule a new cron or run production backfills. ## Linear - Root: CLAW-170 - Children: CLAW-171, CLAW-172, CLAW-173, CLAW-174, CLAW-175, CLAW-176, CLAW-177 ## Verification - `git diff --check` - `bun run ci:static` - `bun run ci:unit` (233 files / 2371 tests) - `bun run ci:types-build` - `bun run ci:packages` - GitHub checks green: static, unit, types-build, packages, e2e-http, playwright-smoke, playwright-local-auth, CodeQL, secret scanning, Socket, Vercel, and repo automation. - `$autoreview` clean for CLAW-176/CLAW-177 closeout paths; no accepted/actionable findings. ## Production Smoke Boundary - `bun run mod -- whoami` against prod passed as `patrick-erichsen-2`. - `bun run mod -- security-scans overview --window-hours 24 --json` against prod returned `No matching routes found`, expected before these new endpoints are deployed. - After merge/manual production deploy, rerun prod overview/list/inspect commands against `https://clawhub.ai`.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
